nfl.headline: NFL offseason board turns toward quarterbacks, rookie roles and roster pressure
nfl.snapshot: 6 NFL analysis cards are active. With the season not yet underway, the board is focused on roster construction, quarterback clarity, coaching installs, rookie roles and injury timelines.
nfl.summary: Quarterback rooms remain the first place the NFL desk should look because starter clarity, rookie timelines, injury recovery and offensive-system fit determine how teams will be framed once camp opens.

Quarterback depth charts are already shaping the next NFL season
Rookie classes move from draft grades into role-and-usage questions
New coaching staffs and system changes are quietly redrawing team expectations
Injury recovery timelines sit near the center of offseason team outlooks
Contract pressure and roster math are becoming football stories before camp
Offensive-line and pass-rush questions may decide which teams travel well into September

why_it_matters: The NFL is currently a news-and-analysis board, not a scoreboard board.

MARKET: NBA playoff spread, total, moneyline and player props.
LINE / ODDS: FanDuel Research lists Spurs -3.5 and total 219.5.
IMPLIED PROBABILITY: check posted moneyline before publication.
MOVEMENT: compare the opener with current prices before tipoff.

cards.why_it_matters: San Antonio faces elimination at home in Game 6 of the Western Conference finals, with Victor Wembanyama and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander anchoring the main player context. NBA.com lists the game for 8:30 p.m. ET; FanDuel Research lists Spurs -3.5 with a 219.5 total, so the spread, total and player-prop screen deserve the first betting read.

stories.title: Injury recovery timelines sit near the center of offseason team outlooks
stories.snapshot: The offseason board should keep injury recovery visible because PUP-list decisions, practice limitations and early camp availability can change depth charts before the first preseason snap.
key_data: Primary lens: rehab progress, practice participation and camp restrictions
Reporting value: availability often becomes the first hard football signal of the summer
Fantasy/betting value: injury uncertainty changes draft rooms, season outlooks and player-prop markets
Calendar context: offseason roster construction and preparation window
why_it_matters: The NFL is currently a news-and-analysis board, not a scoreboard board.
The strongest stories before the season come from roster construction, quarterback clarity, coaching installs, injury timelines and role competition.
what_to_watch: Monitor minicamp, OTA, training-camp and preseason language for role changes and depth-chart movement.
Track injury participation, contract tension, quarterback reps, rookie usage and coaching-system clues.
story_angles: Which starters are racing camp timelines?
Which injured players could begin the year limited?
Which teams need contingency plans if recovery stalls?
